coach.response.energy.header: Based on your assessment, you use **{ac}h of AC**, **{fan}h of fans**, and **{apps}h of appliances** daily. Here are personalized tips:\n\n
coach.response.energy.tip.ac_high: Set your AC to **24°C** instead of lower temps — this alone can save up to 40% cooling energy. Try reducing by {hours} hour(s).
coach.response.energy.tip.ac_fans: Use ceiling fans alongside AC — you can set AC 2°C higher and still feel comfortable.
coach.response.energy.tip.apps_high: Your **{hours}h of appliance usage** is significant. Unplug devices on standby — they consume 5–10% of household energy.
coach.response.energy.tip.led: Switch to **LED bulbs** — they use 75% less energy and last 25× longer.
coach.response.energy.tip.strip: Use a **smart power strip** to automatically cut power to idle devices.
coach.response.energy.tip.efficient: Your energy usage is already efficient! Consider solar panels for the next step.
coach.response.transport.header: You currently use **{mode}** as your primary transport.
coach.response.transport.car.body:
Cars are the highest-emission mode of daily transport. Here's how to reduce your impact:

• **Use public transit 2-3 days/week** — this alone can cut transport emissions by 40%.
• For trips under 3 km, try **walking or cycling** — zero emissions + health benefits.
• **Carpooling** with one colleague halves your per-person emissions.
• If possible, consider switching to an **electric or hybrid vehicle**.
coach.response.transport.bike.body:
Motorbikes emit less than cars but still have room for improvement:

• Switch to **public transit** for longer commutes — trains are 80% more efficient.
• Use a **bicycle** for short trips under 5 km.
• Keep your motorbike well-maintained — proper tire pressure improves fuel efficiency by 3%.
coach.response.transport.bus.body:
Great choice using public transit! To improve further:

• Try **cycling or walking** for trips under 3 km.
• Use **trains** for longer distances — they're even more efficient than buses.
• Consider getting a **bicycle** for your daily commute.
coach.response.transport.green.body:
Your transport choices are already very eco-friendly! 🌟

• Keep it up — your transport emissions are minimal.
• Encourage friends and family to try green transport too.
• For occasional long trips, choose trains over flights when possible.
coach.response.food.heavy_meat.body:
Heavy meat consumption has a high carbon footprint. Here are gradual steps:

• Start with **Meatless Monday** — just one day a week makes a difference.
• Replace beef with **chicken or fish** — beef has 5× the emissions of chicken.
• Try **legume-based meals** (lentils, chickpeas) — they're protein-rich and low-carbon.
• Switching to a mixed diet could **improve your score by ~8 points**.
coach.response.food.mixed.body:
A balanced diet is a good start! To go greener:

• Add **3 fully plant-based days** per week.
• Choose **seasonal, locally-sourced** produce — it has lower transport emissions.
• Reduce **food waste** — the average household wastes 30% of food purchased.
• Going fully vegetarian could **boost your score by ~4-6 points**.
coach.response.food.green.body:
Excellent! A vegetarian diet is one of the most impactful eco-choices. 🌱

• Focus on **local and seasonal produce** to minimize transport emissions.
• Consider reducing **dairy consumption** — dairy has a notable carbon footprint.
• Try **batch cooking** to reduce energy usage in the kitchen.
coach.response.water.high.body:
High water usage contributes to both your carbon footprint and resource depletion:

• Use a **5-minute shower timer** — saves 40+ litres per shower.
• Fix **leaky taps** — a dripping faucet wastes 20 litres/day.
• **Reuse greywater** for plants — kitchen and shower water works great.
• Install **low-flow showerheads** — they reduce water usage by 40%.
coach.response.water.medium.body:
You're in the moderate range. Here are ways to optimize:

• Take **shorter showers** — aim for under 5 minutes.
• Run washing machines and dishwashers only with **full loads**.
• Collect **rainwater** for garden and plant watering.
• Turn off water while **brushing teeth** — saves 8 litres per minute.
coach.response.water.green.body:
Your water usage is already efficient! Great job! 💧

• Maintain your good habits and share tips with others.
• Consider installing a **greywater recycling system** for the garden.
• Monitor your water bill for any unexpected increases (could indicate leaks).
coach.response.score.points_needed: You're **{points} points** away from Green level! Here's your fastest path:\n\n
coach.response.score.already_green: You're already in the Green zone! Here's how to stay there and improve:\n\n
coach.response.score.tip.transport: **Switch to public transport** 2+ days/week → biggest single impact.
coach.response.score.tip.food_heavy: **Reduce meat consumption** → switch to a mixed diet for ~8 points.
coach.response.score.tip.food_mixed: **Add 3 vegetarian days** per week → gain ~4-6 points.
coach.response.score.tip.water: **Reduce water usage** to medium → gain ~6 points.
coach.response.score.tip.maintain: Keep maintaining your eco-friendly habits — you're doing great!
coach.response.score.tip.retake: **Take the assessment again** after making changes to track your progress.
coach.response.greeting:
Hello! 🌱 I'm your **EcoMirror AI Coach**. Your current Eco Score is **{score}/100** ({level}).

I can help you with:
• ⚡ Saving electricity
• 🚗 Reducing travel emissions
• 🥗 Sustainable food choices
• 💧 Water conservation
• 📊 Improving your Eco Score

What would you like to know?
coach.response.fallback:
Great question! Based on your Eco Score of **{score}/100** ({level}), here are your top opportunities:

• Focus on your **highest-emission category** for the biggest gains.
• Even small changes in transport, food, or energy make a measurable difference.
• Try asking me about specific topics like "electricity
coach.motivation.green: 🌟 You're in the Green zone! Keep up your amazing eco-friendly lifestyle.
coach.motivation.near_green: 🚀 You're only {pointsToGreen} points from Green Level — one change could get you there!
coach.motivation.moderate_green: 🌱 You're {pointsToGreen} points from Green Level. Focus on your top category for the fastest improvement.
coach.motivation.far_green: 💪 You're {pointsToGreen} points from Green Level. Let's work together to close that gap step by step.

learn.tip.1: Cycling to work just 3 days a week can save over 1,500 kg of CO₂ per year.
learn.tip.2: A single round-trip flight from New York to London produces about 1.6 tonnes of CO₂ per passenger.
learn.tip.3: Public transit produces 45% less CO₂ per mile than driving alone.
learn.tip.4: If you drive, keeping your tires properly inflated can improve fuel efficiency by 3%.
learn.tip.5: Walking or cycling for trips under 3 km could eliminate 5% of all transport emissions.
learn.tip.6: Beef production generates 60 kg of CO₂ per kg of meat — 20x more than plant proteins.
learn.tip.7: If food waste were a country, it would be the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases.
learn.tip.8: Going vegan for one year saves approximately 1.5 tonnes of CO₂ equivalent.
learn.tip.9: Buying local, seasonal produce can reduce your food's carbon footprint by up to 30%.
learn.tip.10: One-third of all food produced globally is wasted — that's 1.3 billion tonnes per year.
learn.tip.11: LED bulbs use 75% less energy than incandescent bulbs and last 25 times longer.
learn.tip.12: Standby power (phantom load) accounts for 5-10% of residential electricity use.
learn.tip.13: Lowering your thermostat by just 1°C can reduce heating costs by 10%.
learn.tip.14: Solar panels can offset 3-4 tonnes of CO₂ per year for an average home.
learn.tip.15: Washing clothes in cold water can save up to 500 kg of CO₂ per year.
learn.tip.16: The fashion industry produces 10% of global carbon emissions — more than aviation and shipping combined.
learn.tip.17: Buying one second-hand item instead of new saves an average of 5.7 kg of CO₂.
learn.tip.18: A new smartphone generates about 70 kg of CO₂ during manufacturing.
learn.tip.19: Choosing products with minimal packaging can reduce your waste by 40%.
learn.tip.20: Repairing items instead of replacing them can save both money and up to 50 kg of CO₂ per item.
learn.tip.21: The average person's carbon footprint is about 4-8 tonnes of CO₂ per year globally.
learn.tip.22: Trees absorb about 22 kg of CO₂ per year — plant one to offset your daily emissions.
learn.tip.23: The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels.
learn.tip.24: Small daily changes can reduce your carbon footprint by 20-30% within a year.
learn.tip.25: Composting food scraps can divert 30% of household waste from landfills.
learn.article.1.title: The Hidden Cost of Fast Fashion
learn.article.1.content: The fashion industry is responsible for 10% of global carbon emissions. A single pair of jeans requires 7,500 liters of water to produce. By choosing quality over quantity, buying second-hand, or organizing clothing swaps, you can significantly reduce your fashion footprint. Consider a capsule wardrobe approach — fewer, versatile pieces that last longer.
learn.article.2.title: Why Your Diet Matters Most
learn.article.2.content: Food production accounts for 26% of global greenhouse gas emissions. Animal agriculture alone contributes 14.5% of all emissions. Shifting even one meal per day from meat to plant-based can reduce your food carbon footprint by 30-50%. Start with 'Meatless Mondays' and explore cuisines that are naturally plant-forward like Mediterranean, Indian, and East Asian cooking.
learn.article.3.title: The Electric Vehicle Revolution
learn.article.3.content: Electric vehicles produce 50-70% fewer emissions over their lifetime compared to gasoline cars, even accounting for battery production. But the greenest option is still reducing trips altogether. Combining errands, carpooling, cycling for short distances, and working from home when possible can cut your transport emissions dramatically.
learn.article.4.title: Home Energy: Quick Wins
learn.article.4.content: Simple changes at home can reduce energy bills by 20-30%. Start with LED bulbs (75% less energy), smart power strips (eliminate phantom loads), and a programmable thermostat. Washing clothes in cold water, air-drying when possible, and running full dishwasher loads can save hundreds of kWh per year.
learn.article.5.title: Understanding Carbon Offsets
learn.article.5.content: Carbon offsets fund projects that reduce emissions elsewhere — like reforestation, renewable energy, or methane capture. While not a substitute for reducing your own emissions, quality offsets can help neutralize what you can't eliminate. Look for Gold Standard or Verified Carbon Standard certifications. Prioritize reducing first, then offset what remains.
learn.article.6.title: The Impact of Food Waste
learn.article.6.content: One-third of all food produced globally is wasted. When food decomposes in landfills, it produces methane — a greenhouse gas 80x more potent than CO₂ over 20 years. Plan meals, use leftovers creatively, freeze what you can't eat soon, and compost scraps. A family of four can save $1,500/year by reducing food waste.
learn.article.7.title: Water: The Invisible Footprint
learn.article.7.content: Water treatment and distribution consume significant energy. Shorter showers (5 min saves 35 liters), fixing leaks, and using water-efficient appliances reduce both water use and energy. Your 'virtual water' footprint — water used to produce goods you buy — is often 10x your direct usage. A cotton t-shirt needs 2,700 liters of water to produce.
learn.article.8.title: Renewable Energy at Home
learn.article.8.content: Even without solar panels, you can choose renewable energy. Many utilities offer green energy plans at little to no extra cost. Community solar programs let you benefit from solar without installing panels. If you can install, residential solar typically pays for itself in 6-8 years and can reduce your home's carbon footprint by 80%.
learn.article.9.title: The Circular Economy Mindset
learn.article.9.content: Instead of the linear 'take-make-waste' model, the circular economy keeps products in use as long as possible. Repair instead of replace. Buy refurbished electronics. Donate or sell items you no longer need. Choose products designed for durability and repairability. Every item kept in circulation avoids the emissions of manufacturing a new one.
learn.article.10.title: Digital Carbon Footprint
learn.article.10.content: The internet produces about 3.7% of global emissions — similar to the airline industry. Streaming video, cloud storage, and cryptocurrency mining are major contributors. You can help by reducing video quality when HD isn't needed, unsubscribing from unnecessary emails, deleting old cloud files, and choosing green-powered data centers when possible.

results.tips.green.1: Plant a tree to offset even more — every small action compounds.
results.tips.green.2: Consider composting to further reduce your waste footprint.
results.tips.moderate.0: Try walking or cycling for short trips — saves fuel and boosts health.

detective.offender.transport: Daily motor vehicle travel contributes most to your carbon emissions. Switching to public transit or cycling could make a huge difference.
detective.offender.energy: Your electricity consumption, particularly air conditioning, is your biggest emission source. Small reductions add up fast.
detective.offender.food: Your dietary choices contribute the most to your carbon footprint. Reducing meat intake even slightly has a measurable impact.
detective.offender.water: Your water usage is the dominant contributor. Shorter showers and efficient appliances can cut this significantly.

landing.section1.subtitle: Every day, human activities pour billions of tonnes of CO₂ into our atmosphere — and most people have no idea how much they contribute.

landing.problem.callout: The average person emits **4–8 tonnes** of CO₂ per year. **EcoMirror AI** makes the invisible visible — so you know exactly where to act.
landing.section2.title: How EcoMirror
landing.section2.subtitle: A seamless 4-step journey from awareness to measurable impact.
landing.workflow.step1.title: Track
landing.workflow.step1.desc: Log your daily activities — transport, food, energy, and shopping — with our intuitive tracker.
landing.workflow.step2.title: Analyze
landing.workflow.step2.desc: Our Carbon Detective AI identifies your biggest emission sources and hidden opportunities.
landing.workflow.step3.title: Improve
landing.workflow.step3.desc: Get a personalized 4-week action plan and chat with your AI Eco Coach for guided steps.
landing.workflow.step4.title: Reduce
landing.workflow.step4.desc: Watch your Eco Score climb as you complete challenges, earn badges, and track real impact.
